sourcePolicy: Public-safe documentation policy. Contains no private data, extracted assets, proprietary paths, or internal reports.

cards.title: Public Repository Homepage Minimal Policy
cards.summary: Keep public repository homepages focused on product identity, install, usage, and stable capabilities. Do not expose internal planning tracks or detailed private-data boundary lists on the homepage.
designRules: For README homepage content, prefer concise sections for features, install, MCP setup, configuration, project structure, collaboration usage, and license.
Do not generate homepage sections named Roadmap, Private Knowledge, or equivalent internal policy displays.
Do not link internal planning documents from the homepage when the link mainly exposes future-track details rather than user-facing usage.
Keep repository safety policy in implementation guardrails and ignored files; do not turn it into prominent homepage copy.
When updating public docs, scan the homepage for internal roadmap language, private-data policy language, package references, resource-location wording, and raw-report wording before release.
cards.mgaiUsage: When mgai generates or reviews README/homepage documentation, apply this policy before producing the final file. If a homepage contains internal roadmap or private knowledge boundary sections, recommend removing them from README and keeping the homepage user-facing.
